Event Name: Opening Reception of "TINY"
Event Description: FOUND:RE Contemporary and FOUND:RE Phoenix Hotel announce “Tiny,” an exhibition featuring local artists with works 8” x 8” or smaller. “Tiny” is explored through a variety of mediums such as painting, drawing, sculpture, photography, fiber art, printmaking, and more. This exhibition features artwork as little as 1” x 1” to as large as 8” x 8”. Visitors are sure to find something to delight them! “Tiny” features the work of Lando Acosta, Oz Anarchy, Mabry Anderson Andrew Arnt, Alison Auditore, Daphne Azzi, Joseph Bliol, Darcy Borg, Jen Bosen, Carolina Caples, Alberto Carbajal, Tara Catalano, Kara Chaddock, Clarie Chilton, Jacey Coca, Norman Davis, Nick Erickson, Jyll Ethier-Mullen, Gale Goldman (aka Goldwoman), Virginia Gott, Joshua Gridley, Aubree Hansen, Stacy Holmstedt, Lathenia Janae, Sandra Johnson Krause, Kiok Jun, Armida Kielty, Jackson Kloog, Igor Kogan, Dani Kump, Peter Kuttner, Myja Lark, Alex Minwoo Lee, Ellen MacEwan, Lamda Mahrya, Teena Maree, Thomas McKee, Max Myers, Ashley Pinnick, Carey Rasmussen, Revolta Art, Maria Rivas, Emily "Stardust" Roberts, Maxine Rose, Bruce Spiegel, Mohan Toopal, Emily Voigt, Kim Walker, Amber Wanlielista, Karla Watson, Mark Watson, Jeff Weiss, Hristi Wilbur, John Woodruff, and Chad Worth. The exhibition will be hung in the studio space at FOUND:RE Phoenix and opens to the public on April 17th. The show is open through October 11th, with an opening reception on April 17th, from 6-8pm. The exhibition will be presented as part of FOUND:RE Contemporary’s regular schedule of rotating exhibitions. The works will be for sale in person and at

Event Name: 22nd Annual Kappa League Scholarship and Awards Showcase
Event Description: Get ready for the 22nd Annual Kappa League Scholarship and Awards Showcase - a night of celebrating achievements and supporting education! Kappa League is not just an organization; it's a movement dedicated to empowering young men across the valley. Through mentorship, career opportunities, and exposure, Kappa League instills values of leadership, personal growth, and academic excellence in its members. Our program brings together successful men with 6th – 12th grade male students from diverse backgrounds, nurturing their potential and guiding them towards a brighter future. The Annual Kappa League Showcase is the end-of-year celebration highlighting the accomplishments of the Kappa League Scholars. It serves as an opportunity for the Scholars to showcase skills they've learned during the program. It's also an opportunity to raise funds for the Kappa League program to ensure the programs continued success Da’Shaun Holmes, a proud native of St. Petersburg, FL, is a shining example of what focus, drive, and a passion for learning can accomplish. At The Canterbury School of Florida, he distinguished himself early—excelling academically while actively contributing to organizations such as Mu Alpha Theta and the Science National Honor Society. With a clear vision for his future, Da’Shaun pursued Mechanical Engineering at Florida Atlantic University, where his dedication and ingenuity quickly set him apart. In December 2023, he earned his bachelor’s degree and made history at FAU’s Senior Design Showcase, becoming the first student to win both the People’s Choice Award and the Judge’s Choice Award for his innovative senior project. Before crossing the graduation stage, Da’Shaun was selected for a competitive internship with Honeywell Aerospace in Phoenix, AZ, where he trained as an Advanced Manufacturing Engineer. His performance led to a full-time offer in his hometown—but his ambition didn’t stop there. Recognizing where he could make the greatest impact, Da’Shaun returned to Arizona, earning a promotion and continuing to excel in his engineering career. Today, Da’Shaun not only stands as a rising leader in aerospace engineering but also as a proud member of Kappa Alpha Psi Fraternity, Inc., embracing excellence, achievement, and service in everything he pursues
Place: George Washington Carver Museum and Cultural Center

Event Name: Coffee at the Capitol + Lunch & Learn: Lawmaker Panel
Event Description: Join ASBA for a full day of small business advocacy at the Arizona State Capitol. ASBA is Arizona’s small business ally at the State Legislature, working to ensure the voice of small business is heard on the issues that matter most. Start the day with Coffee at the Capitol, a casual outdoor gathering where small business owners, community leaders, legislators, and legislative staff can connect during the current legislative session. This free morning event is a great opportunity to build relationships, have meaningful conversations, and engage directly around the policy issues impacting Arizona’s small business community. Complimentary coffee and networking included. In the afternoon, continue the conversation with our Lunch & Learn: Lawmaker Panel, followed by an Advocacy Training led by ASBA’s new CEO, Steve Kaiser, former Arizona State Senator and public policy expert. This ticketed afternoon session will take place indoors and is limited to 40 attendees on a first-come, first-served basis. Lunch is included with the price of admission, and ASBA members receive a reduced ticket price.

Event Name: Bob Crawford: America's Founding Son
Event Description: From the bassist of the Grammy-nominated band the Avett Brothers, Bob Crawford presents his book, America's Founding Son. During the tumultuous period between the era of the Founding Fathers and the disunion of the Civil War, John Quincy Adams was the man standing in the breach. After an unsuccessful presidential reelection campaign, he was left reckoning with his political legacy. But Adams would be dragged back into the fray in ways he never expected, pitting him against the slavocracy and Southern congressmen and solidifying him as a key ally to the antislavery cause. Event Name: Pediatric International Trauma Life Support Course (PITLS)
Event Description: Phoenix Children’s Trauma Program is sponsoring a Pediatric ITLS course on May 17, 2026. Phoenix Children’s Trauma Program is sponsoring the upcoming Pediatric ITLS (International Trauma Life Support) course, Sunday, May 17, 2026. This course will focus on the special needs of young trauma patients. In 8 hours, you will learn the principles of proper assessment, management, critical interventions, patient packaging and rapid transport. Practice patient assessment and management on actual “live” children at our skill stations. For more information contact Lisa Dowrick, ldowrick@phoenixchildrens.com or 602-933-3817.
